memories.memory_text: I woke up and couldn't see straight—everything was doubled—and when I tried to move, my hands didn't respond right. I barely made it out of bed before I hit the floor.
grounded_facts: Chev Chelios wakes up with blurred or doubled vision.
He experiences delayed or impaired motor control in his hands.
He attempts to get out of bed but falls to the ground.

memories.memory_text: I saw that black VHS tape sitting in front of my TV, wrapped in police tape like some kind of sick joke, and I just grabbed it—couldn't think, just moved.
grounded_facts: Chev Chelios sees a VHS tape with police ribbon tied around it, placed in front of his TV.
He reacts immediately, unwraps it, inserts it into the VCR, and hits play.

memories.memory_text: I heard Ricky Verona’s voice on the TV saying, 'I fucking poisoned you in your sleep,' and suddenly my heart was pounding like it was trying to tear out of my chest.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona appears on a TV screen delivering a pre-recorded message. Verona states he poisoned Chev Chelios while he was unconscious. Chev's heartbeat increases in speed and volume as he becomes aware of the poisoning. Chev flashes a frantic glance around the room upon hearing the message.

memories.memory_text: I was on my knees in front of the TV, the sound of my heartbeat roaring in my ears, and all I could do was stare at the screen as Verona kept talking like it was some kind of joke.
grounded_facts: Chev is on his knees in front of the television set. A high-angle view shows Chev from a hidden camera perspective. The heartbeat sound continues to be prominent and overwhelming. Verona’s taunting message is still playing
addressing Chev directly.

memories.memory_text: I was on the phone when that horn blasted—barely yanked the wheel in time, and my heart went wild, pounding so hard I could see it through my chest.
grounded_facts: Chev is driving and using his cell phone when a near-collision occurs.
A horn blast startles him, prompting a sudden swerve to avoid oncoming traffic.
During the near miss, Chev's heartbeat visibly accelerates and becomes more intense auditorily and visually.

memories.memory_text: I screamed at Eve to get a cell phone, then punched the steering wheel so hard my hand went numb—tires screeched as I took off again, heart slamming like it might burst.
grounded_facts: Chev shouts 'GET A CELL PHONE!!!' from off-screen.
The sound of Chev's car squealing is heard immediately after the shout.
Chev is in his car and driving away from Eve's apartment.

memories.memory_text: I had to make sure that bitch at the answering service understood—Chelios is dead if Miles doesn't call back within the hour. Spelled it out like she was too stupid to get it: D-E-A-D.
grounded_facts: Chev called Doctor Miles' answering service and demanded a callback within one hour.
He explicitly spelled 'D-E-A-D' to emphasize his life-or-death condition.
He expressed urgency and frustration during the phone call.

memories.memory_text: I felt my heart slow down—like it was stopping—then I stomped the gas and the adrenaline kicked in, heart slamming faster, LUBDUB, LUBDUB… barely held on.
grounded_facts: Chev experienced a physical sensation of his heart slowing, followed by a surge in heartbeat after accelerating the car.
He used physical action (stepping on the gas) to stimulate adrenaline and maintain consciousness.
The change in heartbeat was accompanied by intense internal auditory perception of 'LUB DUB' sounds.

memories.memory_text: Who would've thought that little bastard Ricky Verona had the stones to come whack me in my own crib... it's inconceivable.
grounded_facts: Chev Chelios expresses disbelief that Ricky Verona had the nerve to attack him
He refers to the attack location as 'my own crib'
He calls Ricky Verona a 'little bastard' and questions his courage
The reflection occurs during a phone call with Kaylo

memories.memory_text: I told Kaylo to put the word out—Ricky Verona’s name, immediate location, anyone sees him they call me, no excuses.
grounded_facts: Chev commands Kaylo to spread the word that he is looking for Ricky Verona
He demands immediate notification if Verona is spotted
He ends the call abruptly after shouting
Find him!
and shoves the phone into his pocket

memories.memory_text: I was swerving through traffic, heart pounding, when Verona called and said he’d hook up with my girl the second I was dead—sounded like he was already planning it like some sick victory lap.
grounded_facts: Chev is driving erratically while on the phone with Verona.
Verona makes a threatening comment about being with Chev's lover after his death.
The call occurs during a high-speed drive with Chev sweating and panting.

memories.memory_text: I saw the cop lights in the mirror, knew I couldn't stop—not now—and slammed the gas, weaving harder, feeling the car respond as I led them into a full-on chase.
grounded_facts: A squad car appears behind Chev with lights flashing and issues a 'pull over' command.
Chev chooses to evade instead of stopping.
His driving becomes increasingly reckless, initiating a high-speed chase.

memories.memory_text: I was screaming through the mall, tires screeching, missing people by inches, cops on bikes and squad cars right behind me — I could feel my heart hammering like it was trying to punch out of my chest.
grounded_facts: Chev is driving his car through the interior of a shopping mall
He is being pursued by five police units, including CHP motorcycles and squad cars
He is struggling to hear the doctor on the phone due to the chaos

memories.memory_text: I rammed the car into the escalator, didn't stop — just stayed in the driver's seat as the wrecked thing tilted up, carrying me to the second floor like some fucked-up ride.
grounded_facts: Chev crashes his car into an escalator
He remains inside the vehicle as it rides up the broken escalator
The act occurs while he is on a phone call with Doc Miles

memories.memory_text: I could feel my heart slowing, getting weak — started to nod off, so I told the cabbie to crank the music. When he blasted that Billy Ray Cyrus shit, I banged my head like it was Metallica, loud enough to shock my heart back into rhythm.
grounded_facts: Chev feels his heart rate slowing and requests loud music as a response
The cabbie plays 'Achy Breaky Heart' by Billy Ray Cyrus at high volume
Chev physically reacts by banging his head to the music to stimulate his heart

memories.memory_text: Something outside caught my eye — not sure what at first, but it made me snap out of it. Told the cabbie to pull over right then, right there, at that 7-11. Didn't explain, just moved.
grounded_facts: Chev notices something through the window that captures his attention
He abruptly instructs the cabbie to pull over at a 7-11
He exits the vehicle, stating he will return in one minute

memories.memory_text: I slammed the clerk over the counter and told him if he moved, he was dead—then I tore open a trash bag and started shoving every caffeine drink and stim I could find inside.
grounded_facts: Chev physically attacked the store clerk by yanking him over the counter and slamming him face-down
Chev issued a threat: 'You move, you die'
Chev used a trash bag to collect caffeine products including Jolt, Coke, Red Bull, and Starbucks Frappuccinos

memories.memory_text: After stuffing the bag full, I saw some cheap flowers in a bucket—made the fastest double take, grabbed them with a pissed-off look, no idea why.
grounded_facts: Chev noticed flowers in a bucket after filling the bag
Chev made a rapid double take followed by a grouch face
Chev took the flowers despite apparent reluctance or confusion

memories.scene_title: 31、EXT ROOFTOP OF CARLITO'S BUILDING, MINUTES LATER
memories.memory_text: I jumped into Carlito's pool fully dressed, grabbed him underwater, and pointed up — I needed to talk and I wasn't waiting. He actually looked surprised to see me.
grounded_facts: Chev enters the pool fully clothed while Carlito is swimming
Chev meets Carlito face to face underwater and makes a gesture pointing upward
The underwater encounter initiates the confrontation

memories.scene_title: 31、EXT ROOFTOP OF CARLITO'S BUILDING, MINUTES LATER
memories.memory_text: I told Carlito I needed an antidote. He shrugged and said it was Chinese shit — no cure.
grounded_facts: Chev tells Carlito he needs an antidote
Carlito shrugs in response
Carlito states the poison is 'Chinese shit' with no antidote

memories.memory_text: I was barely holding on, head slamming the wheel, trying to lock in what the doc was saying—epinephrine, 10 milligram syringes, a fifth of a dose... couldn't afford to mess it up.
grounded_facts: Chev is on the phone with Doc Miles who instructs him to get epinephrine.
The epinephrine comes in 10 milligram syringes and only a small amount—a fifth—is needed.
Chev is struggling to stay conscious, zoning in and out while driving.

memories.memory_text: Kaylo called, said Verona's brother just walked into Charlie O's—I snapped to, hit the gas and told him I'm there, meet me on 3rd and Flower.
grounded_facts: Kaylo informs Chev that Alex, Verona's brother, entered Charlie O's in downtown L.A.
Kaylo is across the street, getting a taco, and saw Alex enter about two minutes prior.
Chev immediately responds by accelerating, declaring he's on his way and instructing Kaylo to meet him at 3rd and Flower.

memories.memory_text: I came up behind Alex with the butcher knife, silent—he never saw me coming.
grounded_facts: Chev was hidden in the kitchen and emerged after Alex passed the butcher block.
Chev was holding the butcher knife when he attacked.

memories.memory_text: I swung the knife hard and felt it slice through his wrist—his hand dropped with the gun still in it, then I kicked him out into the alley.
grounded_facts: Chev cut off Alex's gun hand at the wrist with a single swing.
Alex's hand and gun fell to the ground together.
Chev kicked Alex through the back door into the alley.

memories.memory_text: I stood there watching Verona freeze when he saw me, then knock over the table—his brother's phone in my hand, the one thing he couldn't ignore.
grounded_facts: Chev Chelios confronts Verona in his home.
Chev possesses Verona's brother's cell phone.
Verona reacts with shock and anger, knocking over a table.

memories.memory_text: I saw the necklace on Alex’s body, yanked it off, and saw the name I. Verona on the back — knew it meant something real personal.
grounded_facts: Chev Chelios sees a necklace on Alex's body.
Chev yanks the necklace off Alex's neck.
The necklace has a silver WWII-era Russian medallion with a mounted Cossack.
The name 'I. Verona' is engraved on the back of the medallion.
Chev recognizes the name Verona and associates it with personal significance.

memories.memory_text: I held up the medallion and told Verona he killed his own brother—watched him realize he'd slipped up, saw the rage flash before he could shut it down.
grounded_facts: Chev Chelios confronts Verona from off-screen, referencing the fratricide.
Chev uses the medallion as leverage, implying it connects to Verona's family and the murder.
Verona reacts with anger and self-reproach after revealing too much about the medallion's lineage.

memories.memory_text: I cut in front of some old guy at the pharmacy counter, my heart pounding, and asked the pharmacist for something starting with 'E'—adrenaline, artificial shit, I knew I needed it but couldn't say the name straight.
grounded_facts: Chev interrupts an old man with a walker to reach the pharmacist
Chev asks for a medication starting with 'E' and describes it as artificial adrenaline
Chev explicitly states he has heart problems

memories.memory_text: Some pimply teenager at the magazine rack said nasal spray has epinephrine—pointed to those little bottles—and I grabbed a handful, seeing the pharmacist on the phone, watching me.
grounded_facts: A teenager informs Chev that nasal spray contains epinephrine
The teenager gestures toward a display of NAS-ALL nasal spray bottles
Chev observes the pharmacist on the phone, looking at him suspiciously
Chev takes multiple nasal spray bottles from the display

memories.memory_text: I saw my face on those TVs in the store window—some fucked-up sketch making me look like a monster, and the news calling me a rampage killer. I just stood there, frozen, while people around me stared at the screen not knowing I was right there.
grounded_facts: Chev sees a police sketch of himself on multiple TVs in a department store window
The sketch is described as exaggerated, simian-browed and thick-lipped, making him look like a serial rapist
A news anchor reports on a man at large responsible for death, injuries, and massive property damage
Chev stops and joins the crowd watching the broadcast
He does not hang up the phone, letting it drop from his ear

memories.memory_text: I told Eve I was fatally poisoned and some psycho was coming to kill her, but she didn't get it—just said she'd be there, like it was nothing. I hung up, shoved that wrecked bike upright, and got the hell out before everything shut down.
grounded_facts: Chev informs Eve that he has been fatally poisoned and that a psychopath may be going to her location.
Eve responds casually, unaware of the danger.
Chev ends the call and struggles to extract a damaged, smoking motorcycle from wreckage.
Chev rides away on the sputtering motorcycle.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Eve, told her I was parked out back, but then saw one of Verona's men at the front door — I had to keep moving, but she stopped, fumbling with her keys, talking about some waffle thing like it mattered. When she finally got the door open, I tried to speak, but my body locked up and I just coughed — couldn't get the words out, just this ragged burst of noise.
grounded_facts: Chev approaches Eve from behind and turns her around in the hallway.
Chev states he is parked out back.
Chev sees a hooded man (Hood #1) approaching the front steps through a security door.
Eve stops in front of her door, causing Chev to nearly collide with her.
Eve mentions forgetting to turn off the waffle iron and searches for her keys.
Eve unlocks and opens the door.
Chev attempts to speak but instead coughs uncontrollably, producing staccato, unintelligible sounds.

memories.memory_text: I told her I had to go, saw the cops coming, and just ran — left her there screaming, but I couldn't stop, not with the blue lights closing in.
grounded_facts: Chev says 'I have to go. Please understand.' before fleeing.
Chev sees a flash of blue light indicating approaching police.
Chev runs away, leaving Eve behind in the street.
Eve screams 'YOU'LL BURN IN HELL FOR THIS!!!' as he leaves.
Chev shouts 'I'll call you!' while running away.

memories.memory_text: I came up behind that hood, grabbed his gun off the ledge, spun him around, and jammed it under his chin—asked him where Kaylo was, voice shaking from the poison but I kept it tight.
grounded_facts: Chev ambushes Hood #2 from behind a ventilation duct
Hood #2 is distracted
leaning over the roof edge with his gun and jacket on the ledge
Chev takes the gun and points it under Hood #2's chin while interrogating him
Chev asks
Yeah I am. Where's Kaylo?

memories.memory_text: He didn't answer fast enough—just started apologizing—so I grabbed his leg, hoisted him over the edge, and tipped him off; heard him scream 'You son of a—' before the thud way below.
grounded_facts: Chev loses patience with Hood #2's hesitation
Chev says
OK
ding
time's up
Chev grabs Hood #2's leg
lifts him over the ledge
and throws him off the roof
Hood #2 falls
yelling
You son of a ... !
before hitting the ground with a thud

memories.memory_text: I don't want some peaceful dream — I want one hour, just one hour of function, to make that bastard pay.
grounded_facts: Chev rejects the doctor's offer of euthanasia with a 'beautiful dream.'
Chev insists on having one hour of enhanced function instead of dying passively.
Chev's expression shifts from brooding to grim determination as he demands the hour.

memories.memory_text: I recorded that message so people would know I don’t want to talk—especially not to strangers trying to sell me something—but still, I hope someone might care enough to leave a real message.
grounded_facts: Eve uses an old tape answering machine instead of a cell phone.
She recorded a message stating she is not home and is uninterested in sales calls.
Her message expresses a conditional openness to hearing from someone if they are not a salesperson.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying 'time's up'—just as Chev started slamming his head into the steering wheel, the sound sharp and sudden, making me flinch in my seat.
grounded_facts: Eve speaks the line 'time's up' in the scene.
Chev begins pounding his head against the steering wheel during the same moment.
The action occurs inside Chev's Audi, exterior setting, same time as previous scene.

memories.memory_text: I left another message—just my usual voicemail greeting—but then Chev screamed like something broke inside him.
grounded_facts: Eve's recorded voicemail message ('Hey, this is Eve ...') plays during the scene.
Chev reacts with a loud, emotional outburst ('AHHHHHHHHHH!') immediately after hearing the recording.

memories.memory_text: I was still half-asleep, stretching and holding the phone with my shoulder when Chev started asking if I’d been sleeping—his voice sounded weird, too intense for this early.
grounded_facts: Eve was sleeping in when the phone call happened.
Eve yawned and stretched during the call.
Eve held the phone between her ear and bare shoulder.
Chev asked if she had been home all day and if she was rested.
Eve responded with minimal words: 'I was sleeping in' and 'Yep.'

memories.memory_text: I was struggling with the microwave, punching buttons, feeling stupid and frustrated, when suddenly there were these loud bangs on the door—I barely looked up at first, but then Chev was there, drenched in sweat, wild-eyed, looking like he’d lost his mind.
grounded_facts: Eve was in the kitchen trying to operate the microwave by pressing random buttons.
There were five loud bangs on the door that initially didn't distract her.
When she opened the door, Chev appeared in a jogging suit, sweaty and wild-eyed.
She physically kept her hands away from him, as if his clothes were contaminated.

memories.memory_text: He ignored me, stormed around checking the bedroom and window, asked if I’d turned on the TV like I was part of some conspiracy, then snapped at me when I tried to ask for help with the clock—I just stood there, stunned, then walked away, feeling insulted and shut out.
grounded_facts: Chev ignored Eve’s attempt to engage about changing the clock.
Chev checked the bedroom and window, acting paranoid.
He asked if she had turned on the TV in a tense, urgent tone.
Eve was in the middle of asking for help with a mundane task when he dismissed her.

memories.memory_text: I picked up the phone, my hands shaking, and said 'Hello?' into the silence.
grounded_facts: Eve was flustered when she answered the phone.
Eve said 'Hello?' after picking up the phone.
There was no response after Eve spoke.

memories.memory_text: I stepped out of my apartment and there he was—Chev—just standing in the hallway, grinning at me like it was normal, blocking the way. I muttered 'I hate that...' under my breath and turned away fast, hoping he’d just disappear.
grounded_facts: Eve exits her apartment and immediately sees Chev in the hallway.
Chev is positioned near the door, obstructing the entrance, and is smiling in an unsettling way.
Eve expresses discomfort with the muttered line 'I hate that...'
She gives Chev a look and begins walking down the hall to avoid him.

memories.memory_text: I was on the ground picking up my stuff after Chev dumped my purse, and I remember the sharp sound of a bullet whizzing over my head—so close I felt the air move—before it hit the window behind me.
grounded_facts: Eve was gathering spilled items from her purse on the ground when the shot occurred.
A bullet passed very close over Eve's head, audible and possibly felt as a disturbance in the air.
The gunshot came from a silenced weapon during a struggle between Chev and an attacker.
Eve did not see the attacker before the shot and was initially unaware of the threat.

memories.memory_text: I just stood there, heart pounding, after that man attacked me—Chev was off killing someone else, and all I could do was say, 'You know, I could use a little help here.'
grounded_facts: Eve was physically present at the rear exit of her building immediately after being attacked by a hood.
Chev was occupied with snapping the hood's neck and disposing of the body when she spoke.
Eve spoke the line 'You know, I could use a little help here' while visibly shaken.

memories.memory_text: I looked around, trying to steady myself, and all I could think to ask was where his car was—then he says he took a cab, like that was even an answer.
grounded_facts: Eve asked Chev, 'Where's your car?' after turning away from the attack scene.
Chev responded that he took a cab, which contrasted with her expectation of immediate escape or preparedness.
Eve rolled her eyes and displayed visible frustration after his response.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying Chev looked ridiculous in those stupid glasses and that outfit, like he didn’t even care how we must’ve looked walking through Chinatown like that.
grounded_facts: Eve comments on Chev's appearance, calling him embarrassing.
Eve questions why they are in Chinatown.

memories.memory_text: I saw his hand—burned and marked like a waffle—and flinched without meaning to. When he said he wasn't a video game programmer, that it was a lie, something in me just... shut down.
grounded_facts: Eve flinches at the sight of Chev's burned, waffle-patterned palm.
Chev admits to Eve that his claim of being a video game programmer was a lie.

memories.memory_text: I flinched when he slammed the chair leg down, and then he said he quit the business for me—like that was supposed to mean something. I couldn’t tell if he was serious or just saying weird things again.
grounded_facts: Eve flinches in response to the sudden noise of Chev slamming the chair leg onto his foot.
Chev states he quit the criminal business for Eve.
Eve responds with 'For me?' and later says 'I don't understand,' indicating confusion.

memories.memory_text: Chev collapsed in front of me, grabbing my arm and begging me to make love to him like it would somehow save his life—I didn’t understand, I just knew he was scaring me.
grounded_facts: Chev staggers after Eve and grabs her arm
Chev falls to his knees, pulling Eve down with him
Chev says 'Make love to me' and 'Save my life'
Eve says 'Chev, you're scaring me'

memories.memory_text: He started tearing at my dress, feeling me up—I hit him in the mouth and we ended up on the ground fighting, me screaming and pounding on him while people watched.
grounded_facts: Chev grabs at Eve's ass
Eve slugs Chev in the mouth
Eve screams and pounds on Chev with her fists
They roll on the ground scratching and clawing
A crowd gathers around them

memories.scene_title: ext thing you know she's kissing and biting his mouth,
memories.memory_text: I was so far gone, begging him to take me right there in front of everyone, my legs in the air—I didn’t care who was watching.
grounded_facts: Eve initiates physical intimacy by fumbling with Chev's pants and encourages public sex. She is described as 'completely out of her head' with eyes closed and legs raised. She says
Take me right here in front of everyone
and 'That's it... do it...'.

memories.scene_title: ext thing you know she's kissing and biting his mouth,
memories.memory_text: I couldn’t believe it—he couldn’t get it up, and then he just answers that damn phone like nothing’s happening; I felt so exposed, so stupid.
grounded_facts: Eve expresses disbelief and frustration when Chev becomes impotent
saying
Tell me you're joking. Now you can't get it up?
After Chev begins to regain function and they continue
she says
God damn it, Chev ...!
When Chev answers his cellphone mid-act
Eve says
What are you doing?!!
indicating shock and humiliation.

memories.memory_text: I remember yelling at Chev, 'What's the matter with you?!'—he just zipped up his pants like nothing was wrong, then told me to shut up.
grounded_facts: Eve speaks the line 'What's the matter with you?!!' in a furious and disbelieving tone.
Chev responds to Eve with 'Shh' after finishing zipping up his pants.
The interaction occurs on a street in Chinatown, immediately after Chev adjusts his clothing.

memories.memory_text: Chev grabbed me, held me back hard—his arm like a bar across my chest—and his voice was so cold, telling me not to let that guy out of my sight, like I had any choice in the matter.
grounded_facts: Chev physically restrained Eve by holding her back with one arm.
Chev gave Eve a direct order to keep surveillance on a man.
Eve responded with a hesitant, emotional 'OK, Chevy...'.

memories.memory_text: I remember Chev rummaging through my purse again, like I wasn’t even there—just grabbing my money to pay that cab driver without a word.
grounded_facts: Chev dug through Eve's purse to retrieve money to pay the Haitian cab driver.
Chev had been holding Eve's purse in his lap during the cab ride.
Eve was not present in the scene, but her purse was used as a resource by Chev.

memories.scene_title: 108、INT DON KIM'S SHIRT FACTORY, 4TH FLOOR, SECONDS LATER
memories.memory_text: I screamed at that little supervisor, 'DON'T TALK TO ME LIKE THAT!! MY BOYFRIEND KILLS PEOPLE!!!'—my voice cracked, my chest was tight, I couldn't breathe right, but I had to make him back off.
grounded_facts: Eve shouted at the supervisor in English after he confronted her and Chev.
She referenced Chev's violence to assert dominance and deflect fear.
Her outburst occurred amid chaos, with workers panicking and the supervisor yelling in Korean.

memories.memory_text: I reached for him, my hands shaking—not from fear, but from something sharper, hotter—like if I didn’t do this, he’d vanish right in front of me.
grounded_facts: Eve initiates physical contact with Chev by reaching for his crotch after understanding adrenaline is needed to slow the poison. She says
Come on, let's finish what you started,
indicating intentional action to revive him. Her behavior shifts from emotional cracking to determination
showing agency in the moment. The act is framed as urgent and life-saving
not purely sexual.

memories.memory_text: I saw the steam, the broken glass, the bodies. I felt sick. I asked if they were okay, then how he could do that. He didn’t answer—he just checked his body and left the car again.
grounded_facts: Eve sees the crashed sedan with steam and broken glass after Chev shoots the pursuers.
Eve is visibly pale and emotionally affected.
She asks, 'Are they... OK?' and then 'How can you... how can you do that...?'
Chev dismisses her concern, focused on his body and next actions.
Chev exits the car again after noticing something, telling Eve to wait.

memories.memory_text: I saw Chev sitting there with blood everywhere, pounding nails into his leg like it was nothing—he didn’t even flinch, just looked up at me and said he couldn’t feel it.
grounded_facts: Eve followed a trail of blood to find Chev in the hardware store.
Chev had already hammered six framing nails into his leg and was driving in a seventh.
Chev stated, 'I can't feel my leg,' while looking at Eve.

memories.memory_text: The phone started ringing—this loud, jarring beep—right in the middle of it all, and I just froze, waiting to see what would happen next.
grounded_facts: A cell phone ringtone (BEE-DEE-DEE-DEEE-DEE-DEEE-DEE-DEE-DUM) played suddenly in the store.
The sound startled both Chev and Eve, breaking the silence after the traumatic sight.
Eve did not speak or move in response to the sound.

memories.memory_text: I said, 'I hate television,' just staring at the screen, not really seeing it. I could feel Chocolate looking at me, like I was crazy, but I didn’t care.
grounded_facts: Eve is sitting on a mattress in Chocolate's apartment, facing a TV.
She delivers the line 'I hate television' with no inflection.
Chocolate reacts by looking at her sideways, implying discomfort or judgment.
The scene emphasizes Eve's flat affect and emotional disengagement.

memories.memory_text: I looked up at Chev, my voice quiet but needing to know—'Do you promise?'—and for a second, everything else just faded.
grounded_facts: Eve asked Chev 'Do you promise?' in a moment of emotional vulnerability.
The scene took place in dim, pale sunlight filtering through half-rolled blinds outside Chocolate's apartment.
Eve was looking into Chev's eyes when she asked the question.

memories.memory_text: I heard Chev's voice on the machine—so calm, like he was already gone—and when he said 'you were the greatest, baby,' I froze, hand halfway to my mouth, not knowing whether to cry or scream.
grounded_facts: Chev left a voicemail message on Eve's answering machine.
In the message, Chev expressed regret and affection, saying Eve was 'the greatest, baby'.
Eve was present in the apartment and heard the message play.

memories.memory_text: I sat on Chev's bed, pointing at the camera, telling him he's dead—watching him lie there unconscious, knowing the poison was already working inside him.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona is on a TV screen, sitting on Chev Chelios's bed while Chev is unconscious behind him.
Verona addresses Chev directly through the recording, taunting him with the knowledge that he has been poisoned.
The scene was recorded hours before, with hoods present in the room and Chev visibly pale.
Verona mentions resisting the urge to dismember Chev but chose poisoning instead.

memories.memory_text: Chev said my name after I hit him. He called me a son of a bitch, said he couldn’t believe I had the nerve to come after him. Now he’s hunting me.
grounded_facts: Chev refers to Ricky Verona as 'that son of a bitch' in the scene
Chev expresses disbelief that Ricky Verona had the courage to attack him
Chev orders Kaylo to put out a call to find Ricky Verona
Chev threatens to find and confront Ricky Verona, possibly before dying

memories.memory_text: He screamed 'Find him!' like I was prey, like I wasn’t the one who already ended him. But I heard it—the desperation. He’s coming, and I know it won’t be quiet. Good. Let him try.
grounded_facts: Chev explicitly commands 'Find him!' referring to Ricky Verona
Chev vows to find and confront Ricky Verona, possibly at the cost of his own life
The command is issued urgently, with intensity, before Chev ends the call

memories.memory_text: I hear Chev screaming my name like a madman, pressing a gun to Orlando's head, demanding to know where I am — feels good knowing he's that desperate, that out of control.
grounded_facts: Chev barges into the Black Sabbath clubhouse and points a gun at Orlando, demanding to know Ricky Verona's location.
Ricky Verona is not present in the scene but is the subject of Chev's violent interrogation.
The outburst occurs while Orlando is surrounded by members of the Black Sabbath gang.

memories.memory_text: I told Chev I’m gonna hook up with his girl the second he’s dead—watching him seethe over the phone was pure gold.
grounded_facts: Verona taunted Chev during a phone call, stating his intention to be with Chev's girlfriend after Chev's death.
The conversation occurred via split screen while Chev was driving erratically.
Verona referenced a previous message left for Chev, calling it a 'gay James Bond tape.'

memories.memory_text: I dropped the news that Carlito’s on my side now—could hear the disbelief in his voice, couldn’t admit it even when I called him out on it.
grounded_facts: Verona revealed to Chev that Carlito had defected and was now loyal to him.
Chev responded with an insult about Verona’s brother, deflecting the claim.
The exchange happened during the same phone call, before Chev received another incoming call.

memories.memory_text: I picked up the phone and said 'Talk to me, bro.' Someone answered, pretending to be Alex, asking if I wanted to suck him off or let him lick my ass.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona answered a phone call with the phrase 'Talk to me, bro.'
The caller, impersonating Alex, said: 'Hey Ricky, whadya think about sucking me off, ya in the mood? Maybe let me lick your ass or sumtink?'
The interaction occurred over the phone, with Verona only hearing the voice, not seeing the speaker.

memories.memory_text: I was feeding my dog, minding my own business, when that voice came through the phone—Chelios, alive and taunting me, saying he had my brother's cell phone. I remember the rage burning up my chest, knocking the table over, pushing the dog away, because I knew then he was coming for me.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona was feeding his Rottweiler beef jerky when he received a call.
Chev called Verona and revealed he had Alex Verona's cell phone.
Verona reacted with fury, knocking over a table and pushing the dog away.
The call directly implied Alex had been overpowered or killed.

memories.memory_text: I was sure he was dead, but then I heard his voice—calm, mocking—asking for more of the poison like it was a fucking joke, and for a second, I couldn't even form a threat.
grounded_facts: Verona believed Chev should be dead.
Chev speaks from off-screen, taunting Verona by saying he likes the poison.
Verona stammers, unable to complete a threat.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying the medallion came from my father, from my grandfather—shit, I wasn't supposed to say that much, but it just slipped out when he had it in his hands.
grounded_facts: Verona mentions the medallion was passed from his grandfather to his father and then to him.
He realizes he revealed too much and immediately tries to deflect with aggression.

memories.memory_text: When Chev said I killed my own brother, I froze—just for a second—then all I could do was spit 'You—' before everything cut to black.
grounded_facts: Chev accuses Verona of killing his own brother.
Verona responds with a stunned 'You—' before the scene cuts away.

memories.memory_text: I looked down and saw Alex's hand, frozen like it was still holding the gun—cold, stiff, staring up at me like some fucked-up joke. Then I looked up at my crew and said it plain: let's go get the bitch.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona saw Alex's severed hand in a trigger-pulling position from his point of view.
Verona shifted emotionally before giving a calm command to pursue vengeance.
He addressed his crew after processing the sight of the severed hand.

memories.memory_text: I don't know how I got here, but I'm tied up and Kaylo's got me—can't move, can't reach my phone, and that psycho Chev is coming.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona is in Kaylo's custody as reported in the scene
Verona is not present in the scene and has no dialogue or actions described
The scene implies Verona is captured and unable to communicate

memories.memory_text: I picked up the phone and said, 'What's up, corpse,' just to see if he'd flinch—then watched my crew stifle laughs when I mocked him about the deal.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona answers his phone and says
What's up
corpse
upon seeing Chev Chelios is calling. He covers the mouthpiece and makes eye contact with his crew
who suppress laughter. He speaks sarcastically and tauntingly
maintaining dominance in front of his crew.

memories.memory_text: After he mentioned the jewelry, I had to pull the phone away—felt the rage burn—but I didn't snap it then; I waited, clenched my jaw, and said 'Hmmm,' then 'I'll be there,' like I was still in control.
grounded_facts: Chev mentions the jewelry taken from Verona's brother
which visibly affects Verona. Verona pulls the phone away
looks at it like he wants to smash it
then composes himself. He responds with a restrained
Hmmm" and later "I'll be there
before sitting in silence, seething. ],

personal_insult
emotional_control
vengeful_focus
tension

memories.memory_text: I stood my ground and told Chelios straight—'I'm nobody's little bitch'—while Carlito was sizing up that ephedrine pump, making sure everyone knew I wasn't backing down.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona says 'I'm nobody's little bitch' in response to Chev's taunt.
The ephedrine pump has been taken from Chev and placed on the table.
Carlito is present and handling the device while observing the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I remember shouting 'He'll pay what I tell him to pay...!' right before things went black—something about the prayer slipping out, then the pain, but I don't remember losing control.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona says 'He'll pay what I tell him to pay...!' during the confrontation.
The phrase 'Ourfather whoartinheaven hallowedbethy' appears in the scene, suggesting Verona begins reciting the Lord's Prayer under duress.
The scene implies a sudden shift from dominance to physical vulnerability, though the exact moment of disarming is not described in this excerpt.

memories.memory_text: I was crawling, trying to gather up what was left of my fingers, and I found a syringe—jammed it in my teeth like a damn pirate because I wasn’t going down empty-handed.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona is crawling on the floor after losing fingers.
He finds a syringe and grabs it in his teeth.

memories.memory_text: I didn’t even see it coming—just felt the blast tear through my foot, another chunk of me blown away, toes this time, while Chev laughed and kept shooting.
grounded_facts: Chev fires at Verona's feet, blowing off a set of toes.
The act is described as compounding earlier finger loss.

memories.memory_text: I jammed the syringe all the way into Chev's neck from behind—felt it hit bone—and watched him drop to his knees, twitching like a puppet with cut strings. His heartbeat was so slow it made me laugh.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona injected Chev in the back of the neck with a syringe.
Chev dropped to his knees and began twitching after the injection.
Verona taunted Chev immediately after injecting him.

memories.memory_text: I took Chev's gun and put three rounds in Carlito's chest—his face was pure shock—then kicked him aside and jumped in the chopper, pressing the barrel to the pilot's head. Told him to fly like he meant it.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona took the gun from Chev's hand.
Verona shot Carlito three times, killing him.
Verona entered the helicopter and forced the pilot to fly at gunpoint.

memories.scene_title: 132、EXT THE SKY ABOVE LOS ANGELES, SECONDS LATER
memories.memory_text: I was fighting to keep control of the chopper, shoving Chev back as he clawed his way into the seat, but he just kept coming, and those damn news copters were swarming like vultures.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona was in the helicopter struggling with Chev Chelios.
Verona attempted to push Chev out of the back seat.
News helicopters were present and filming the midair fight.

memories.scene_title: 133、EXT THE SKY ABOVE LOS ANGELES, SIMULTANEOUS
memories.memory_text: I was winning, telling him he was dead, but then he grabbed my neck and we fell—now I'm falling, he's still choking me, and I can't breathe, can't move, everything's going dark.
grounded_facts: Ricky Verona declared 'You're dead' repeatedly while fighting Chev Chelios midair.
Chev Chelios grabbed Verona by the neck and strangled him during a free fall after both fell from a helicopter.
Verona's body went limp and glassy-eyed before being released.
